page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, providing consistent processing performance.

The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

Given its compute-focused resource ratio of 1:2 (vCPU to RAM) and dedicated core allocation, the ecs.c2.large is designed for workloads requiring sustained CPU performance without massive memory footprints. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive application servers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring consistent execution without resource contention. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage solutions. Designed for compute-heavy tasks, the ecs.c2.large is suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and application hosting where dedicated CPU performance is prioritized over high memory capacity.
